page: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core metrics falling into the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations showing average results. However, the server excels in highly parallelized tasks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests both rank in the top 10% tier. PassMark CPU overall, encryption, and physics tests all demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%.

Memory performance is mixed, with small-block cached read bandwidth showing strong top-25% performance, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths remain average. Database operations, static web serving, and small-model LLM prompt processing perform at average levels, though Redis SET operations achieve strong throughput. The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated single-core execution, but offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage the 64 physical cores.

This server is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, parallel data decompression, and high-throughput caching layers. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-optimized server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, it delivers 64 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, alongside a bundled 300 GB local SSD. While single-core benchmarks like stress-ng fall in the bottom 10%, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-threaded performance, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark extended instructions ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks and Redis SET operations are also strong. The shared CPU allocation makes this instance a qualitatively cost-effective choice for highly parallelized tasks, though it is less suited for single-threaded workloads. It is highly recommended for software compilation, ray tracing, and parallel data processing.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-optimized KVM server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-core-count compute instance optimized for parallel processing, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 300 GB local SSD storage and KVM hypervisor
Top-tier multi-threaded decompression and extended instruction performance
Strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis operations
Qualitatively cost-effective shared CPU model for highly parallel workloads
